data.title: BlackRock Q1 earnings estimated to increase despite drawdowns
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) has seen its Q1'25 EPS estimates revised downwards on high drawdowns, but is nonetheless expected to report higher year-over-year earnings on Friday, April 11, before market open.

The consensus EPS estimate is $10.21 (+4.12% Y/Y) and the consensus revenue estimate is $5.31B (+12.36% Y/Y). Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system grades BLK's Growth as B- and Profitability as A.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen two upward revisions and nine downward revisions. Revenue estimates have seen one upward revision and five downward revisions. Quant grades Revisions as D+.

Last quarter

The world's largest asset manager reported Q4 earnings that beat the consensus estimate, helped by record inflows boosting its assets under management.

Q4 long-term quarterly total net inflows of $281B increased from inflows of $221B in the prior quarter, bringing 2024's total inflows to a record $641B.

BlackRock's assets under management of $11.55T climbed from $11.48T at the end of Q3 and $10.01T at the end of 2023.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $11.93, topping the average analyst estimate of $11.24, rose from $11.46 in Q3 and $9.66 in Q4 2023.

Revenue of $5.68B, exceeding the $5.60B consensus estimate, increased from $5.20B in the previous quarter and $4.63B a year ago.

Analyst comments

"Market enthusiasm and risk-on behavior have tempered over the course of the quarter as economic/market uncertainty has increased, resulting in increased volatility. Looking forward, we expect volatility to persist and expect to see a gyrating market from here," said analysts at Citi Research in a report on North American brokers and asset managers.

"We prefer names that are exhibiting improving/stable core growth trends and lower levels of crypto and capital markets sensitivity. For most names, we have reduced our earnings estimates due to market-driven and/or capital markets revisions," added the research note.

"We decrease our estimates the most for BLK by -8.1% (on lower performance fees that we revised to be more in line with Q1'20/Q1'23 as opposed to Q1'24 which was elevated due to higher LTPC)," said analysts at Morgan Stanley Research in a note on assets managers.

The investment bank lowered its Q1'25 EPS estimate to $10.24 from $11.13, citing that the macro volatility and recent drawdowns drive fund NAVs lower with softer organic growth vs. prior estimates. Average Q1'25 EPS estimate across asset managers was reduced by 3% on lower AUM levels with soft markets and flows.

However, Morgan Stanley maintained its Overweight rating on BlackRock, given the breadth of capabilities to capture outsized share of money in motion in the current volatile backdrop.

"Expect BLK benefit as cash eventually comes off the sidelines once uncertainty abates. Better organic growth supported by broad product set, enviable brand, distribution reachand scaled Aladdin technologies that supports 17% EPS CAGR 2025-27e, that's trading at compelling valuation of 2x-turn discount to the broader market," said Morgan Stanley.

What to expect

BLK is down ~16% year-to-date.

"The last time BlackRock reported earnings, the stock experienced a sound daily performance of 5.19% after beating both earnings and revenue. Back in Q4'24, markets were generally close to all-time high levels, experiencing moderated levels of volatility," noted Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Urbina in an April 7 report.

"Nonetheless, in Q1'25, after the 21st of February, the backdrop completely changed, and the quarterly drawdown was as high as -10% in the S&P 500," added Urbina.

"Currently, the stock is facing a drawdown of approximately -24%, which is half of the maximum drawdown of 2022, and forward valuations have reduced significantly, passing from 24.8x to 17.5x. Yet, this forward valuation will likely decrease as potential downward earnings revisions are expected due to the landscape in global markets," according to the author, who gives the stock a Hold rating.

Seeking Alpha authors and the Quant Rating system grade BLK as Hold. Quant assigns the stock a score of 3.42 on a scale of 5, with grades of D- for Valuation and C+ for Momentum.

Meanwhile, the Wall Street analysts see the stock as Buy.

data.title: RFK Jr. says will know by Sept. what is causing autism
data.description:
Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who has often linked vaccines to the neurodevelopmental disorder autism, said Thursday that his agency will know by September the reason behind its rising prevalence in the U.S.

During a Cabinet meeting attended by President Donald Trump at the White House, RFK Jr. added, “At your direction, we've launched a massive testing and research effort that's going to involve hundreds of scientists from around the world.”

“By September, we will know what has caused the autism epidemic, and we will be able to eliminate those exposures,” he added.

His remarks follow the CDC’s recent plans to run a study to investigate whether vaccines cause autism, a claim debunked by numerous peer-reviewed studies, including a 2015 research published in JAMA that evaluated the health records of more than 95K children.

Vaccine makers: GSK (NYSE:GSK), Merck (NYSE:MRK), Moderna (MRNA), BioNTech (BNTX), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Novavax (NVAX), and Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY).

data.title: Google in spotlight as tech giant signs deal with US gov't to cut software prices
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) was in the spotlight on Thursday as the tech giant signed a deal with the U.S. government to cut the prices of its business software.

The U.S. General Services Administration said it reached a deal with the Mountain View, Calif.-based Google to temporarily cut the price of its Workspace software — which includes Gmail, Contacts, Calendar, Meet and Chat — by 71% for every federal agency, regardless of how large the deal is. 

The discounts are effective until the end of the federal year, Sept. 30. The deal comes at a time when the Trump administration is seeking to slash government expenses.

Microsoft (MSFT) is the dominant enterprise software provider for the U.S. government, with its suite of Office software.

“Google will now approach the federal government as one unified customer — and President Trump’s GSA is working hard on this collaboration to turn that recognition into real savings to secure lower prices for best-in-class technology across all federal agencies,” said GSA Acting Administrator, Stephen Ehikian in a statement. “By working closely with industry leaders like Google, we will continue to lower the cost of IT while providing improved experiences for the American taxpayers and the federal government.”

“We were eager to work with GSA on this innovative way to bring Google’s technology to more federal customers, because lower costs and better performance can go hand-in-hand,” Karen Dahut, CEO of Google Public Sector, added. “GSA’s ‘One-Customer’ approach will help modernize IT procurement, and we are proud to offer the potential of billions in savings to federal agencies.”

The GSA, which has been targeted by the Department of Government Efficiency for cost reductions, is responsible for more than $110B in products and services via federal contracts.

data.title: Hugo Barra, who worked at Meta, Google, and Xiaomi, joins Sonos' board
data.description:
Wireless sound system solutions company Sonos (NASDAQ:SONO) has appointed former vice president of Meta's (META) Oculus VR division, Hugo Barra, to its board of directors.

Barra, whose appointment was effective April 7, was also a VP at Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY). Prior to that, he was VP for Android product management at Google (GOOG) (GOOGL).

Sonos also said that its longtime director, Mike Volpi, was resigning. He was on the board for over 15 years.

The key appointment comes after several top-level exits. Earlier this year, marketing chief Jordan Saxemard, product chief Maxime Bouvat-Merlin, and CEO Patrick Spence left the company.

The senior-level exits came in the wake of failure to improve the customer experience for the company's products and delays for new ones.

While still CEO last year, Spence admitted his hastiness to redesign the company's app fell short of the mark.

data.title: JPMorgan, Bank of America face Canada meeting after Trump's complaints - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) and Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) are among the U.S. banking behemoths invited by the Canadian government for a meeting, the details of which have yet to be determined, according to a media report dated Thursday.

The news comes after President Donald Trump's complaints that U.S. firms face hurdles doing business north of the border. 

Representatives from the investment banks face a meeting with officials from Canada’s Department of Finance, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter. Timing and other details of the gathering are still being worked out, they added.

Despite Trump's recent claim that U.S. banks can't operate in Canada, over a dozen -- including JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) and Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) -- actively operate there, though Canadian lenders lead in retail banking and many other lines of business, the article said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: US Steel falls as Trump says he likes Nippon Steel as an investor
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) dropped 6% partly as President Donald Trump said he likes Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) as an investor and doesn't want the US steelmaker sold to a Japanese company, 

"For a foreign country to buy that cherished U.S. Steel brand, that's hard for me," Trump said during a cabinet meeting at the White House. "Why can't they just build a plant themselves. Why do they have to take US Steel."

Trump said he feels "better about" Nippon Steel being an investor rather than owner of US Steel (NYSE:X).

“US Steel is going to do very well now because of the tariffs,” Trump said. “I don’t know why they even need a deal.”

Trump's latest comments come after remarks on Wednesday where he said he didn't want  US Steel (NYSE:X) taken over by a Japanese company. 

"We love Japan but US Steel is a very special company," Trump said.

data.title: Wall Street tempers expectations for banks' Q1 earnings
data.description:
With the spike in economic uncertainty, investors are likely to be attuned to banks' guidance when they report Q1 earnings, starting on Friday.

Morgan Stanley analyst Betsy Graseck expects managements to lower revenue for FY2025 during Q1 earnings. "With recent market volatility, clearly the outlook for investment banking and wealth fees needs to be tempered," she wrote in a note to clients. The firm's equity analysts lowered their median 2025 Y/Y revenue growth estimates to 3% from 4%. For those banks that provide loan growth guidance, expect that to decline, too, she added.

And for Q1 itself, after two "beat and raise" quarters in a row for large cap banks, Morgan Stanley now expects a  "meet and keep" quarter.

Given the volatility in markets, "many investors wonder what will even matter this EPS season, as any positive prints will likely be seen as backward looking and stable-ish outlooks may be met with skepticism," wrote UBS analyst Erika Najarian.

For Q1, note that most banks in the table are expected to see earnings rise from a year ago. However, for most banks, consensus estimates have been reduced in both the past month and three-month period. 

As Evercore ISI analyst Glenn Schorr wrote in a note about Q1 earnings, "Not too much to get too excited about this quarter, with the only real hope for any earnings beats to be driven by really strong trading at the brokers."

"On the bank side, we see low growth in loan & deposit trends, stable enough expense & credit trends and strong trading results that will help offset the known pauses in M&A and IPOs," he said.

J.P. Morgan analyst Vivek Juneja expects:

  • Q1 net interest income down at most banks Q/Q, on average, led by day-count impact;
  • Loans remaining weak;
  • Investment banking revenue slightly softer than expected, but trading revenue up solidly Y/Y;
  • Wealth management fees up Q/Q;
  • Mortgage banking revenue to benefit from higher origination revenue but still at low levels;
  • Controlled core expense trends;
  • Credit losses continuing to normalize and mixed loan-loss reserve trends; and 
  • Continued share buybacks at most banks.

J.P. Morgan reduced Q1 EPS estimates for most of the large-cap and large regional banks it covers. However, the firm increased its Q1 EPS for U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) by a penny and kept Q1 estimates for Regions Financial (NYSE:RF) and Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC) unchanged. For FY2025, all EPS estimates were trimmed, with core EPS seen dropping 5% Y/Y.

Morgan Stanley's favored names  heading into Q1 earnings are JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM); rated Equalweight, Citigroup (NYSE:C), rated Overweight; and BNY (NYSE:BK), rated Overweight.

J.P. Morgan favors the global systemically important banks heading into Q1 over regionals. Citigroup (NYSE:C) should benefit near term on higher trading revenue. Juneja's Overweight names include: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ:FITB), PNC Financial (NYSE:PNC), and Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN).

Evercore ISI's Schorr likes BNY (NYSE:BK) due to its conservative guidance. Meanwhile, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) and Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) are "best-in-breed" consumer and commercial banks that have upside to a resolution of economic turmoil.

UBS's Najarian is focused on stocks with long-term tailwinds, including Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) and Capital One Financial (COF). She flags notable potential stock movers in response to Q1 earnings to be Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), American Express (AXP) and KeyCorp (KEY).

data.title: Vale raised to Buy at BofA as bottom-up story has improved significantly
data.description:
Vale (NYSE:VALE) +1.8% in Thursday's trading, bucking the day's widespread losses on Wall Street, as Bank of America upgrades shares to Buy from Neutral with an $11.50 price target, up from $11 previously, saying the stock's discounted valuation after recent losses combined with the company's improved bottom-up story offer enough margin of safety to accommodate a more cautious iron ore view.

Vale's (NYSE:VALE) "bottom-up story has improved significantly," BofA says, after overcoming several overhangs including CEO succession, Mariana settlement and the railway dispute; the CEO succession was concluded with a new and highly-regarded mnagement team including the strong duo of CEO Gustavo Pimenta and CFO Marcelo Bacci, the Mariana settlement was finalized with new provisions below market expectations, and the railway dispute ended.

While not all overhangs are gone - the U.K. lawsuit is still looming - BofA believes the bottom-up story is now on significantly better footing, meanwhile, the bank says this is the first time in some years that Vale's (VALE) production and cost guidance is undeniably conservative, and discussions with investors have consistently been to the tune of Vale beating it and not missing.

Valuation-wise, BofA sees Vale (VALE) trading at 4.2x EV/EBITDA and generating 8.5% yield in2025, which the bank believes is attractive, especially given its 2025 iron ore price forecast of US$96/ton, only slightly above its long-term price forecast.

data.title: Wall Street craters a day after historic rally as Nasdaq slides 5%, S&P sheds 4%
data.description:
Wall Street's euphoria disappeared on Thursday as market participants were back in selloff mode. 

The three major averages steadily fell until hitting a session low just after noon, following which they recouped some losses. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) briefly threatened a stock circuit breaker after falling as much as -6.26%. It was last -4.08% at 5,234.39 points in late afternoon trade.    

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -4.80% at 16,303.10 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -3.04% at 39,372.65 points.  

Stock circuit breakers are implemented by exchanges to halt trading in cases of a rapid decline. Divided into three levels, the first level stops trading if the S&P 500 falls 7% intraday. The second level kicks in at 13%, and the third level at 20%. The last circuit breaker occurred during the height of the COVID-19 stock market crash in March 2020.   

On Wednesday, U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on most reciprocal tariffs except for those against China sent the S&P (SP500) to its best day since the 2008 financial crisis. The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) logged its second-best day ever. 

But traders on Thursday came to grips with the fact that, even with the 90-day pause, it was just a short-term relief from trade war escalation and that longer-term recession risks were still very much real.

"Even with the 90-day postponement of the punitive tariffs we retain our call that the U.S. economy will slip into a modest recession that will look like a garden variety inventory correction that will last between 6 to 9 months. There are simply too many simultaneous shocks that are moving through the economy for it to slip the surly bonds of recessions gravity," Joseph Brusuelas, principal and chief economist at RSM US LLP, said on X (formerly Twitter).

The White House confirmed that the tariff rate against China is now actually 145%, not 125% as conveyed on Wednesday. The continued trade war escalation between the world's two biggest economies stoked concerns.

"The stock market is taking a breather after Wednesday's outlandish rally, as investors start to realize that even with the pause, a trade war with China is still happening, with very little clarity on the path ahead between the two nations.  It is common after big up days to see a pullback. The market is trying to find its footing after a week of whiplash," Robert Ruggirello, chief investment officer at Brave Eagle Wealth Management, said. 

Even a downside surprise in a key inflation gauge did little to brighten the mood. Before the opening bell, data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics showed a -0.1% M/M decrease in the March consumer price index (CPI), compared to an expected figure of +0.1%. Core CPI came in at +0.1% M/M, lower than the +0.3% consensus. 

However, economists see the March print as the final reading before the effects of tariffs start showing up.   

"No obvious evidence of tariffs in the March numbers even though China 20% and some others already in effect then. But many more tariffs started in April and some have lag to prices. So expect inflation to pick up in the coming months," Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said on X.   

Over in the fixed-income markets, a rally in bonds extended to a second straight day. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 3 basis points to 4.36%. The shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 9 basis points to 3.86%.  

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: BBB Foods projects 26-29% revenue growth and up to 550 new stores for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BBB Foods Inc. (TBBB) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Anthony Hatoum highlighted strong operational results, including the opening of 138 new stores in Q4 2024 and 484 for the full year, exceeding the initial guidance. Total revenues for Q4 increased by 32.7% to MXN16.3 billion, and full-year revenues reached MXN57.4 billion, a 30.3% year-over-year growth.
  • Hatoum emphasized the company’s ability to outperform market trends, with same-store sales growth of 11.8% in Q4, compared to ANTAD's 2.6% growth during the same period.
  • CFO Eduardo Pizzuto reported Q4 EBITDA of MXN845 million, representing a 51% increase year-over-year. Full-year EBITDA grew to MXN2.8 billion. He noted that SG&A expenses increased by 96 basis points in Q4, driven by non-recurring expenses and investments in talent and compliance.
  • Hatoum announced plans to open between 500-550 new stores in 2025 and projected same-store sales growth of 11%-14%, with total revenue growth expected to range between 26%-29%.

Outlook

  • Management provided guidance for 2025, projecting same-store sales growth between 11%-14% and total revenue growth in the range of 26%-29%. Store openings are forecasted to range from 500 to 550.
  • Hatoum noted that operational efficiencies and a consistent focus on value proposition improvements will continue to drive growth in 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenues increased by 32.7% to MXN16.3 billion, while full-year revenues reached MXN57.4 billion, up 30.3% year-over-year.
  • Same-store sales growth for Q4 was 11.8%, and for the full year, it was 13.4%.
  • Operating cash flow for the year improved by 19.4% to MXN3.749 million, with a net cash position of approximately MXN1.4 billion as of year-end.
  • Private label sales represented 54% of total sales in 2024, up from 47% the previous year, contributing significantly to revenue growth.

Q&A

  • Bob Ford, Merrill Lynch, asked about growth balance in new and existing trade areas. Hatoum explained that expansion focuses on increasing store density where the company operates while stretching into new geographies.
  • Joseph Giordano, JPMorgan, inquired about same-store sales composition. Hatoum attributed growth to higher store traffic and increased ticket size, despite price deflation in private labels.
  • Alejandro Fuchs, Itaú, asked about gross margin trends. Hatoum explained that improvements in scale and supplier relationships drive gross margins, though quarterly fluctuations are expected due to pricing strategies.
  • Froylan Mendez, JPMorgan, asked about the feasibility of introducing ultra-fresh products. Hatoum stated that any new category, such as fruits and vegetables, would need to meet strict operational and value criteria before launch.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in understanding the company's ability to sustain its growth trajectory, with questions focusing on store expansions, same-store sales dynamics, and gross margin trends.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout the call, emphasizing operational resilience and the ability to thrive in various economic conditions. Hatoum frequently reiterated the company's focus on value proposition improvements and operational scalability.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained similarly optimistic, with consistent confidence in achieving future growth targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 saw an acceleration in store openings, with 138 stores added compared to 131 in Q3. Full-year store openings exceeded the 2023 total by 21%.
  • Same-store sales growth of 11.8% in Q4 was slightly above 11.6% in Q3, demonstrating sustained performance.
  • Revenue growth in Q4 was 32.7%, compared to 29.8% in Q3, reflecting stronger seasonal performance and operational efficiency.
  • Management's strategic focus on private label expansion and operational efficiency remained consistent.

Risks and Concerns

  • Increased SG&A expenses due to non-recurring costs and investments in talent and compliance were noted as challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential impacts of macroeconomic conditions on consumer behavior and competition from other grocery retailers.
  • Management emphasized the resilience of the business model and the ability to adapt to economic fluctuations.

Final Takeaway

BBB Foods Inc. reported strong Q4 and full-year 2024 results, driven by robust store expansion, same-store sales growth, and private label penetration. With plans to open up to 550 new stores and achieve 26%-29% revenue growth in 2025, the company remains confident in its ability to scale and deliver value to customers amidst economic uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
